prevalence.source: European neurological epidemiology syntheses / IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging demographics and improved survival

burden.mortalityRate: Increases with age; indirect mortality common
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / WHO Europe

vulnerablePopulations: people with limited access to specialist care in underserved regions
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: MDS clinical diagnostic criteria; imaging (DaTscan/MRI) used when diagnostic uncertainty exists; multidisciplinary staging and follow-up

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; high access to specialist diagnosis in many member states
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Generally good symptom control with medication and multidisciplinary care; advanced therapy access is common in many countries

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist neurology and movement disorder clinics widely available; standardized diagnostic pathways
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Levodopa-based therapy, MAO-B inhibitors, dopamine agonists; multidisciplinary rehab; DBS and infusion therapies available in many tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising burden with aging and variability in access between member states and rural vs urban settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong specialist networks and access to advanced therapies and multidisciplinary rehabilitation
